expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: donut-chart data label coverage distinguishes label visibility, density, and overlap-sensitive outcomes
expectations.evidence: The output explicitly states the focus cannot be shown as covered: “Result: NOT SATISFIED (advisory) — The benchmark focus cannot be shown to be explicitly covered using only the provided blind pre-defect evidence.” It also notes missing evidence for “label visibility rules”, “label density behavior”, and “overlap/collision handling”, and no Phase 4a draft artifact is available to confirm coverage.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: The run does not provide a Phase 4a subcategory-first scenario draft; instead it reports an inability to verify alignment: “No Phase 4a draft artifact … is provided in evidence, so Phase 4a alignment cannot be directly verified.” The produced result.md is an evaluation narrative rather than a Phase 4a contract-conforming artifact (no subcategory-first scenarios with atomic steps/observable leaves).
